BifucationDiagrams

Christopher Moore

10/5/2021

  • One-dimensional bifurcation diagram
    • One parameter value
    • Many parameter values

One-dimensional bifurcation diagram

One parameter value

  # Parameters
    N0 <- 0.25
    lambda <- 1.4
  
  # Time
    steps <- 100
    step_seq <- 1:steps
  
  # Data structure
    N_vec <- rep(x= NA, times = steps)
  
  # Loop
    N_vec[1] <- N0
      for (i in 2:steps) {
      Nt <- N_vec[i-1]
      N_vec[i] <- Nt*lambda*(1 - Nt)
      }

  # Plot  
    plot(x = step_seq, y = N_vec, xlab = "Step", ylab = "N", las = 1)

Many parameter values

  # Parameters
  N0 <- 0.25
  lambda_min <- 0.5
  lambda_max <- 4
  n_lambdas <- 2000
  lambda_vec <- seq(from = lambda_min, to = lambda_max, length.out = n_lambdas)
  
  # Time
  steps <- 1000
  step_seq <- 1:steps
  
  # Data structure
  N_mat <- matrix(data = NA, nrow = steps, ncol = n_lambdas)
  
  # Loop
  N_mat[1,] <- N0
  for (j in 1:n_lambdas) {
    lambda_j <- lambda_vec[j]
    for (i in 2:steps) {
      Nt <- N_mat[i-1, j]
      Nt1 <- lambda_j*Nt*(1 - Nt)
      N_mat[i, j] <- Nt1
    }
  }

n_last_steps <- 100
last_steps <- (steps-n_last_steps + 1):steps
x_lim <- c(lambda_min, lambda_max)
y_lim <- c(0, max(N_mat))

plot(x = NA, type = "n", xlab = "Lambda", ylab = "N", xlim = x_lim, ylim = y_lim, las = 1)
  for (k in 1:n_lambdas){
    lambda_k <- lambda_vec[k]
    points(x = rep(x = lambda_k, times = n_last_steps), y = N_mat[last_steps,k], pch = 16, col = "#00000011", cex = 1/3)
  }
